planned_actions: 1. Create src/cli/src/lib/schema-template.ts with generateTemplate() and listSchemas() exports. generateTemplate resolves $ref inline (external via DEFS_SCHEMA, self via rootSchema.$defs), recurses with depth guard (max 10), emits required-only fields with placeholder values per design guide format.

2. Create src/cli/src/commands/schema.ts with registerSchemaCommands(program). Two subcommands: schema list (outputs sorted JSON array of schema names), schema template <type> (outputs generated template JSON, accepts --role for evidence conditional schemas).
3. Wire into src/cli/src/main.ts: add import and registerSchemaCommands(program) call.

4. Handle evidence.schema.json conditional schemas: when --role provided, iterate allOf entries, match if.properties.role.const to --role value, union then.required with top-level required, generate merged template.
5. Handle record.schema.json self-referencing $defs/tierResult: detect #/$defs/X pattern, resolve from rootSchema.$defs[X].
6. Handle edge cases: oneOf -> first variant, additionalProperties with type -> {}, type array -> null, enum -> first value, const -> const value.
7. Build and verify all eval commands pass.
edge_cases: oneOf items (record challenge_review.concerns) - pick first variant
type array like [string, null] - produce null
additionalProperties with schema type - produce empty {}
evidence --role with invalid role value - error with available roles
Schema with minItems >= 1 array - generate one item
Nested required-only filtering at every depth level
Depth guard at 10 to prevent infinite recursion
non_goals: Modifying existing schema.ts (Ajv validation) - template logic is separate concern
Adding schema validation to the template output
Supporting arbitrary custom schemas not in SCHEMAS registry
demo_steps: Build succeeds: cd src/cli && npm run build
geas schema list outputs JSON array of all schema type names (15 entries from SCHEMAS)
geas schema template task-contract outputs valid JSON with all required fields filled with placeholders
geas schema template evidence outputs base evidence template (version, agent, task_id, role, summary)
geas schema template evidence --role implementer outputs evidence template with files_changed included
geas schema template evidence --role reviewer outputs evidence template with verdict and concerns included
geas schema template record outputs record template - tier_results with self-ref tierResult resolved
$ref to _defs.schema.json resolved: producer_type shows first agentType enum value, created_at shows iso-utc-timestamp placeholder
geas schema template nonexistent-type produces error with available types list
geas schema template evidence --role invalid produces error with available roles

known_risks: eval_commands in task contract use tsc build only, not esbuild bundle - plugin/bin/geas may need separate bundle step
record template only shows version and task_id since other sections are optional - could be surprising to users
untested_paths: Schema types with deeply nested $ref chains beyond 2 levels
Schema with both oneOf and $ref in same node
self_check.summary: Implementation covers all 6 acceptance criteria. Build passes (tsc), all 9 test scenarios verified manually. $ref resolution handles external (_defs), self-ref ($defs/tierResult), and conditional (allOf/if/then) patterns correctly. Depth guard at 10 prevents infinite recursion. Code is clean, follows existing patterns, no Ajv dependency.

tier_1.details: All eval_commands pass, all acceptance criteria met after bug fix

tier_2.details: Design authority approved, QA found bug which was fixed and re-verified

closure.change_summary: Added geas schema command group with two subcommands: schema list (outputs sorted JSON array of 15 schema type names) and schema template <type> (generates fill-in JSON template with required-only fields, $ref resolution, conditional schema support via --role). Created schema-template.ts with recursive schema walker, commands/schema.ts for commander registration, updated main.ts. Bug found during QA (role value not reflecting --role flag) was fixed.

reviews.summary: All 6 design guide criteria verified: separate module, no Ajv, recursive resolution, required-only, correct placeholders, conditional merge
Found role value bug, fixed. All acceptance criteria pass after fix.

open_risks: eval_commands use tsc only, bundle step (npm run bundle) needed separately

verdict.rationale: All 6 acceptance criteria verified passing. QA-found bug (role value not reflecting --role flag) was fixed and re-verified. Design authority approved both contract and implementation. Additive feature with no regression risk. Clean scope: 3 files added, no unrelated changes. Worker confidence 4/5. Challenger review appropriately skipped for normal-risk additive work.

what_went_well: Design guide was detailed and accurate - implementation followed it closely
QA caught a real bug (role value not reflecting --role) that would have been user-facing
Separate schema-template.ts module kept concerns clean
All acceptance criteria verified with concrete manual tests
what_broke: Role field in template not reflecting --role parameter - the enum-first-value logic was correct for general case but missed the special case where the role option should override
QA initially reported changes_requested - required a fix cycle
what_was_surprising: tsc build does not update plugin/bin/geas - bundle step is separate (npm run bundle). eval_commands in task contract only had tsc, not bundle
rule_candidates: eval_commands should include both build and bundle steps: cd src/cli && npm run build && npm run bundle
memory_candidates: schema-template.ts is the template generation module, separate from schema.ts (Ajv validation)
When generating templates with role/variant selection, the selected variant value must override the enum default in the output
next_time_guidance: Always include npm run bundle after npm run build in eval_commands for CLI tasks
When adding --option flags that select a variant, ensure the option value is reflected in the template output, not just the conditional field merging
